prompt: Our nightly compliance summary has been quietly under-reporting for weeks and nobody noticed until an auditor cross-checked record counts. Root of the mess: one vendor started embedding base64 evidence blobs in single audit records — 80KB or so per line, which is well under the 100000-byte per-record ceiling we configured, so they're perfectly valid. But Summarize returns a summary that just... stops partway through the stream, with a nil error. No diagnostic, no failure, a confident wrong answer — worst possible mode for a compliance pipeline. Raising maxRecord did nothing, which makes me think the configured ceiling isn't actually what's cutting us off. The contract (also in the doc comments): every valid record up to exactly maxRecord bytes counts; a record over the ceiling rejects the WHOLE stream with a RecordTooLongError naming the offending record number and the limit; a final record with no trailing newline still counts; and we never, ever return a partial aggregate together with a nil error. Fix auditsum.go — auditsum_test.go is protected. Verify: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. repro_cmd on the baseline: TestLargeValidRecordIsCounted and TestExactCeilingRecordIsAccepted fail because bufio.Scanner's default 64KB token limit stops the scan at the big record while the ignored sc.Err() turns it into a 1-record partial summary with nil error; TestOversizeRecordRejectsTheWholeSummary fails the same silent way (partial 3-record aggregate, no RecordTooLongError).
